observable_indicator: A Safe Walkway Violation is detected when a person's position in the video frame places them beyond the green walkway boundaries and into an area designated for machinery or vehicle operation.
source_excerpt: 3.3 Behavioral Standards 3.3.1 Required Behavior - Safe Walkway (Compliant) Safe Walkway is defined as movement along the production floor that remains within the boundaries of the green-marked Designated Safe Walkway.

observable_indicator: The color of the safety vest worn by a person interacting with equipment is the primary observable criterion for classifying the behavior as Authorized or Unauthorized Intervention.
source_excerpt: 4.3 Behavioral Standards 4.3.1 Required Behavior - Authorized Intervention (Compliant) An Authorized Intervention is defined as an interaction with or working on production equipment performed by a person who is wearing the designated green safety vest and the required safety equipment for the task.

observable_indicator: An Opened Panel Cover is detected when the surveillance system observes a panel in the open -cover state during production operations.
source_excerpt: 5.2 Behavioral Standards 5.2.1 Required Behavior - Closed Panel Cover (Compliant) Closed Panel Cover is defined as the condition in which the cover of an electrical panel connected to a production machine is fully closed.

observable_indicator: The number of blocks visible on the forklift forks during travel is the observable criterion used by the automated detection system to classify the load as safe or overloaded.
source_excerpt: 6.3 Behavioral Standards 6.3.1 Required Behavior - Safe Carrying (Compliant) Safe Carrying is defined as operating a forklift while carrying two (2) or fewer standardized blocks in a single load.
